question_text: Two identical springs are arranged in series (one after the other). When a 10 N force is applied to the combination, the first spring extends by 4 cm and the second by 4 cm. What would be the extension of a single spring under a 10 N force?
explanation: In series, both springs experience the same force (10 N), so they have the same spring constant. Since each extends by 4 cm under 10 N, a single spring would also extend by 4 cm under the same force. The total extension of 8 cm results from two springs in series, each contributing equally.
